code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    # Updated phrase map with more reference‑friendly wording and object‑first patterns
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "The elevation of {s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "The elevation of {s} is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "The elevation of {s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "The airport is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "The airport is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {s} is {o} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "The runway length of {s} is {o} feet",
        "runwayName": "The runway at {s} is designated {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the first runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "The length of the first runway at {s} is {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{s} has first runway number {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway surface at {s} is {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "The second runway surface at {s} is {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the third runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "The third runway surface at {s} is {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the fourth runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fourth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "{s} has fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fifth runway surface at {s} is {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "{o} is the ICAO location identifier of {s}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{o} is the location identifier of {s}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "{o} is the IATA location identifier of {s}",
        "nativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "{s} is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "{o} is the capital of {s}",
        "language": "{o} is an official language of {s}",
        "leader": "{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "officialLanguage": "{s} has the official language {o}",
        "order": "{s} belongs to the order of {o}",
        "class": "{s} belongs to the class {o}",
        "division": "{s} belongs to the division {o}",
        "regionServed": "{s} serves the region of {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s} has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "{s} has mayor {o}",
        "city": "{s} is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "{s} falls under jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": "The demonym of {s} is {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "{s} operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "{s} operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        # (additional mappings can be added here as needed)
    }

    # Group phrase fragments by subject to build a compact description
    subject_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object.strip().strip('\"').strip('\'')
        # surface type values are usually nouns; render them in lower case for natural phrasing
        if t.predicate.endswith("SurfaceType"):
            obj = obj.lower()
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            # Render full phrase then strip leading subject if present
            phrase = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=obj)
            if phrase.startswith(t.subject):
                phrase = phrase[len(t.subject):].lstrip()
        else:
            phrase = f"{t.predicate} {obj}"
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build a sentence for each subject, joining its fragments with commas and 'and'
    sentences = []
    for subj, parts in subject_map.items():
        if len(parts) == 1:
            combined = parts[0]
        else:
            combined = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]
        sentences.append(f"{subj} {combined}")

    # Join different subjects with semicolons
    sentence = "; ".join(sentences) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
